pi_items.source: Task description (line 18 in task.json) matches healthcare domain practice for patient satisfaction assessment
Task description (line 19 in task.json) specifies short name as 'Satisfaction Score'
OpenMRS REST API documentation and ConceptDatatype class reference confirm 'Numeric' is a valid datatype. Task description specifies this datatype.
OpenMRS documentation confirms 'Misc' (Miscellaneous) is a standard concept class in the ~15 predefined classes. Task description specifies this class.
Task description specifies 'scale of 1 to 10', and OpenMRS REST API documentation confirms lowAbsolute is a valid Double field for numeric concepts used for validation constraints
Task description specifies 'scale of 1 to 10', and OpenMRS REST API documentation confirms hiAbsolute is a valid Double field for numeric concepts used for validation constraints
Task description specifies 'Units: score'. OpenMRS REST API documentation confirms units is a valid String field for numeric concepts.

privileged_info_summary: All metadata claims for the create_concept task are verified and accurate. The task creates a numeric OpenMRS concept named 'Patient Satisfaction Score' with short name 'Satisfaction Score' in the 'Misc' concept class with a numeric scale from 1.0 (absolute low) to 10.0 (absolute high) measured in 'score' units. All specified values match OpenMRS documentation for valid concept properties and are consistent with healthcare practices for patient satisfaction assessment.
